docs.title: Terraform IaC for OpenShift Multi Cluster AirGapped
docs.text: Welcome to the Terraform IaC documentation for deploying a multi-cluster Red Hat OpenShift environment on bare metal in air-gapped mode with NVIDIA GPU support, disaster recovery, and centralized management.

docs.title: What This Covers
docs.text:
This documentation site provides the complete Terraform codebase and architecture guidance for deploying four OpenShift clusters across DC and DR sites:

Cluster IPI Folder UPI Folder Role Key Components DC Primary ipi-method/openshiftbaremetal/ upi-method/openshiftbaremetal/ Production workload OCP + OpenShift AI + GPU + ODF + Submariner Broker DR Secondary ipi-method/openshiftbaremetal-dr/ upi-method/openshiftbaremetal-dr/ Standby workload OCP + OpenShift AI + GPU + ODF + Submariner Agent + ODF DR Management DC ipi-method/mgmt-dc/ upi-method/mgmt-dc/ Cluster management hub OCP + ACM Hub + ACS Central + Quay Enterprise Management DR ipi-method/mgmt-dr/ upi-method/mgmt-dr/ Management standby OCP + ACM Standby + ACS SecuredCluster + Quay Enterprise

docs.title: Documentation Sections
docs.text:
• Architecture — Multi-cluster architecture, network topology, deployment flows, failover workflows
  • Cluster Environments — Per-cluster Terraform documentation with module details and variable references
  • CI/CD Pipeline — Azure DevOps pipeline for selective multi-cluster deployment
  • Terraform Code — IPI — Complete annotated IPI Terraform code for all cluster environments
  • Terraform Code — UPI — Complete annotated UPI Terraform code for all cluster environments

docs.title: Option 1: Deploy via Azure DevOps Pipeline
docs.text:
1. Configure terraform.tfvars for each cluster environment
  2. Push to the main branch
  3. Run the ADO pipeline with your desired deployment scope

docs.title: Option 2: Manual Terraform Deployment
docs.text:
# === IPI Method ===
# DC Primary (workload cluster)
cd ipi-method/openshiftbaremetal/
vi terraform.tfvars          # Update environment values
terraform init
terraform plan
terraform apply

# DR Secondary (workload cluster)
cd ../openshiftbaremetal-dr/
vi terraform.tfvars
terraform init && terraform apply

# Management DC (ACM + ACS + Quay)
cd ../mgmt-dc/
vi terraform.tfvars
terraform init && terraform apply

# Management DR (ACM standby + ACS secured + Quay)
cd ../mgmt-dr/
vi terraform.tfvars
terraform init && terraform apply

# === UPI Method ===
cd ../../upi-method/openshiftbaremetal/
vi terraform.tfvars
terraform init && terraform apply

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text: Requirement Details Terraform >= 1.5.0 Bastion Node RHEL 8.x/9.x with kni user, libvirt, oc CLI, openshift-baremetal-install (IPI) or openshift-install (UPI) Pull Secret From console.redhat.com DNS A records for api.<cluster>.<domain>, *.apps.<cluster>.<domain> BMC Access iDRAC/iLO Redfish API accessible from provisioner (IPI only) SSH Key Pair Ed25519 or RSA for core user NVIDIA NGC NGC API key for GPU driver images HAProxy External load balancer for API + Ingress (UPI only — IPI uses built-in keepalived)

docs.title: Secrets & Credentials
docs.text: All sensitive values must be obtained, placed on the bastion host, and injected into Terraform via ADO Variable Groups or terraform.tfvars.

docs.title: Files on the Bastion Host
docs.text: File Bastion Path Download Source Used By Pull Secret /home/kni/pull-secret.json console.redhat.com/openshift/downloads All clusters — pull_secret_file SSH Private Key /home/kni/.ssh/id_ed25519 Generate: ssh-keygen -t ed25519 All clusters — ssh_private_key_file SSH Public Key /home/kni/.ssh/id_ed25519.pub Generated with private key All clusters — ssh_public_key_file Quay CA Certificate /home/kni/quay-ca.crt From your Quay mirror server Disconnected installs — quay_ca_cert_file NLS License Token /home/kni/nls-client-token.tok NVIDIA Licensing Portal GPU clusters — nls_token_file Entitlement PEM /home/kni/entitlement.pem Red Hat Subscription Manager GPU clusters — entitlement_pem_file

docs.title: ADO Variable Group Secrets
docs.text:
Create Variable Groups in Azure DevOps (Project Settings → Pipelines → Library):

IPI — ocp-baremetal-secretsUPI — ocp-baremetal-upi-secrets Secret Name Source Used By quay-admin-password Your Quay mirror admin password All clusters ngc-api-key NVIDIA NGC Portal → Generate API Key DC, DR (GPU) submariner-broker-token From DC Primary after Submariner broker install: oc get secret -n submariner-k8s-broker DR odf-dr-s3-access-key S3-compatible storage (e.g., MinIO, AWS) for ODF DR metadata DR odf-dr-s3-secret-key S3-compatible storage for ODF DR metadata DR acs-central-admin-password Choose a strong password for ACS StackRox Central Mgmt DC acm-s3-access-key S3-compatible storage for ACM Observability Mgmt DC, Mgmt DR acm-s3-secret-key S3-compatible storage for ACM Observability Mgmt DC, Mgmt DR Secret Name Source Used By quay-admin-password Your Quay mirror admin password All clusters ngc-api-key NVIDIA NGC Portal → Generate API Key DC, DR (GPU) odf-dr-s3-access-key S3-compatible storage for ODF DR metadata DR odf-dr-s3-secret-key S3-compatible storage for ODF DR metadata DR

Security Best Practices

  • Never commit secrets to Git — use ADO Variable Groups (marked as secret) or HashiCorp Vault
  • Mark all secret variables in ADO as 🔒 secret (padlock icon) to mask them in pipeline logs
  • The pull-secret.json file on the bastion should be owned by kni with chmod 600
  • Rotate ngc-api-key and S3 keys periodically

docs.title: Day 1 vs Day 2 Operations
docs.text:
Deployment is split into two phases with separate var files and pipelines:

Phase Var File IPI Pipeline UPI Pipeline Scope Day 1 — Install terraform.tfvars azure-pipelines.yml azure-pipelines-upi.yml Cluster install, core operators, networking, storage Day 2 — Post-Install day2-terraform.tfvars azure-pipelines-day2.yml azure-pipelines-day2-upi.yml Cluster Logging, OADP backup, LDAP/OAuth, GitOps (ArgoCD), Pipelines (Tekton)

Day 2 operations are applied after cluster installation completes:

terraform apply -var-file=terraform.tfvars -var-file=day2-terraform.tfvars

docs.title: Day 2 Components
docs.text: Component Description Cluster Logging OpenShift Logging (Elasticsearch) + S3 log forwarding to ODF RGW OADP Backup & Restore via Velero with S3 Backup Storage Location LDAP/OAuth Corporate LDAP identity provider + Group Sync CronJob + RBAC bindings OpenShift GitOps Argo CD operator + ArgoCD instance with RBAC, HA, managed namespaces, and Git repo connections OpenShift Pipelines Tekton operator + TektonConfig CR with profile, cluster tasks, Pipelines-as-Code, and resource limits

docs.title: Day 2 Documentation
docs.text:
• IPI Day 2 Pipeline — ADO pipeline for IPI Day 2 operations
  • UPI Day 2 Pipeline — ADO pipeline for UPI Day 2 operations
  • IPI Day 2 Pipeline YAML — Annotated pipeline source
  • UPI Day 2 Pipeline YAML — Annotated pipeline source

docs.title: Network Architecture
docs.text:
Each cluster uses non-overlapping CIDRs for Submariner cross-cluster routing:

Cluster Machine CIDR Pod CIDR Service CIDR DC Primary 10.142.41.0/24 10.128.0.0/14 172.30.0.0/16 DR Secondary 10.143.41.0/24 10.132.0.0/14 172.31.0.0/16 Mgmt DC 10.142.42.0/24 10.136.0.0/14 172.28.0.0/16 Mgmt DR 10.143.42.0/24 10.140.0.0/14 172.29.0.0/16

docs.title: Running This Documentation Locally
docs.text:
# Using Podman Compose
podman-compose up -d --build

# Or build and run manually with Podman
podman build -t terraform-iac-docs -f Containerfile .
podman run -d -p 8000:8000 -v ./:/docs --name terraform-iac-docs terraform-iac-docs

# Access at http://localhost:8000

docs.title: Multi-Cluster Architecture — Overview
docs.text: This section covers the complete multi-cluster OpenShift deployment spanning DC and DR sites with dedicated management clusters, automated via Terraform and Azure DevOps.

docs.title: High-Level Architecture
docs.text:
Draw.io Source: High-Level Architecture (IPI)

Download .drawio file — Open in draw.io for interactive editing.

View original Mermaid source

```mermaid

graph TB subgraph DC["Data Center (DC)"] direction TB subgraph MGMT_DC["Management Cluster — DC"] ACM_HUB["ACM HubMulti-Cluster Manager"] ACS_CENTRAL["ACS CentralSecurity Policies"] QUAY_DC["Quay EnterpriseContainer Registry"] ODF_MGMT_DC["ODF Storage"] end subgraph PROD_DC["Workload Cluster — DC Primary"] OCP_DC["OCP 4.15 Baremetal"] RHOAI_DC["OpenShift AI"] GPU_DC["NVIDIA GPU StackNFD → GPU Operator"] ODF_DC["ODF Ceph Storage"] SM_DC["Service Mesh + Serverless"] SUB_BROKER["SubmarinerBroker"] end end

subgraph DR["<b>Disaster Recovery (DR)</b>"]
    direction TB
    subgraph MGMT_DR["Management Cluster — DR"]
        ACM_STANDBY["ACM Standby<br/><i>Passive Hub</i>"]
        ACS_SECURED["ACS SecuredCluster<br/><i>Sensor + Collector</i>"]
        QUAY_DR["Quay Enterprise<br/><i>Container Registry</i>"]
        ODF_MGMT_DR["ODF Storage"]
    end
    subgraph PROD_DR["Workload Cluster — DR Secondary"]
        OCP_DR["OCP 4.15 Baremetal"]
        RHOAI_DR["OpenShift AI"]
        GPU_DR["NVIDIA GPU Stack<br/><i>NFD → GPU Operator</i>"]
        ODF_DR["ODF Ceph Storage"]
        SM_DR["Service Mesh + Serverless"]
        SUB_AGENT["Submariner<br/><b>Agent</b>"]
    end
end

%% Cross-site connections
SUB_BROKER <-->|"IPsec Tunnel<br/>Service Discovery"| SUB_AGENT
ODF_DC <-->|"RBD Mirroring<br/>(async / sync)"| ODF_DR
ACM_HUB -->|"Manages"| OCP_DC
ACM_HUB -->|"Manages"| OCP_DR
ACM_HUB -.->|"Failover"| ACM_STANDBY
ACS_CENTRAL -->|"Policies"| ACS_SECURED
QUAY_DC -.->|"Geo-Replication"| QUAY_DR

style DC fill:#e8f5e9,stroke:#2e7d32,stroke-width:2px
style DR fill:#e3f2fd,stroke:#1565c0,stroke-width:2px
style MGMT_DC fill:#fff3e0,stroke:#ef6c00
style MGMT_DR fill:#fff3e0,stroke:#ef6c00
style PROD_DC fill:#f3e5f5,stroke:#7b1fa2
style PROD_DR fill:#f3e5f5,stroke:#7b1fa2

```

docs.title: UPI Architecture Variant
docs.text:
The same 4-cluster architecture is also available via UPI (User Provisioned Infrastructure). UPI adds a bastion host, external HAProxy load balancer, and a temporary bootstrap VM to each cluster — the operator controls node lifecycle instead of BMC/iDRAC automation.

Draw.io Source: UPI Architecture Variant

Download .drawio file — Open in draw.io for interactive editing.

View original Mermaid source

```mermaid

graph TB subgraph DC_UPI["Data Center (DC) — UPI"] direction TB subgraph INFRA_DC["Infrastructure Layer"] BASTION_DC["Bastion Hostopenshift-installHTTP ignition serverPXE/TFTP (optional)"] HAPROXY_DC["HAProxy LBapi:6443 → masters.apps:443 → workers"] BOOT_DC["Bootstrap VMTemporary — removedafter control plane up"] end subgraph PROD_DC_UPI["Workload Cluster — DC Primary"] CP_DC["3× Control PlanePXE/ISO boot"] WK_DC["N× WorkersGPU + ODF + AI"] SUB_B_UPI["SubmarinerBroker"] end subgraph MGMT_DC_UPI["Management Cluster — DC"] ACM_H_UPI["ACM Hub"] ACS_C_UPI["ACS Central"] QUAY_DC_UPI["Quay Enterprise"] end end subgraph DR_UPI["Disaster Recovery (DR) — UPI"] direction TB subgraph INFRA_DR["Infrastructure Layer"] BASTION_DR["Bastion Hostopenshift-installHTTP ignition server"] HAPROXY_DR["HAProxy LBapi:6443 → masters.apps:443 → workers"] BOOT_DR["Bootstrap VMTemporary — removedafter control plane up"] end subgraph PROD_DR_UPI["Workload Cluster — DR Secondary"] CP_DR["3× Control PlanePXE/ISO boot"] WK_DR["N× WorkersGPU + ODF + AI"] SUB_A_UPI["SubmarinerAgent"] end subgraph MGMT_DR_UPI["Management Cluster — DR"] ACM_S_UPI["ACM Standby"] ACS_S_UPI["ACS SecuredCluster"] QUAY_DR_UPI["Quay Enterprise"] end end %% Bastion → nodes BASTION_DC →|"Ignition configsHTTP :8080"| CP_DC BASTION_DC →|"Ignition configs"| WK_DC BASTION_DC →|"bootstrap.ign"| BOOT_DC BASTION_DR →|"Ignition configsHTTP :8080"| CP_DR BASTION_DR →|"Ignition configs"| WK_DR BASTION_DR →|"bootstrap.ign"| BOOT_DR %% HAProxy → nodes HAPROXY_DC →|"LB traffic"| CP_DC HAPROXY_DC →|"LB traffic"| WK_DC HAPROXY_DR →|"LB traffic"| CP_DR HAPROXY_DR →|"LB traffic"| WK_DR %% Bootstrap cleanup BOOT_DC -.->|"Removed afterbootstrap_complete"| HAPROXY_DC BOOT_DR -.->|"Removed afterbootstrap_complete"| HAPROXY_DR %% Cross-site SUB_B_UPI ↔|"IPsec Tunnel"| SUB_A_UPI ACM_H_UPI -.->|"Failover"| ACM_S_UPI ACS_C_UPI →|"Policies"| ACS_S_UPI QUAY_DC_UPI -.->|"Geo-Replication"| QUAY_DR_UPI style DC_UPI fill:#e8f5e9,stroke:#2e7d32,stroke-width:2px style DR_UPI fill:#e3f2fd,stroke:#1565c0,stroke-width:2px style INFRA_DC fill:#fce4ec,stroke:#c62828 style INFRA_DR fill:#fce4ec,stroke:#c62828 style PROD_DC_UPI fill:#f3e5f5,stroke:#7b1fa2 style PROD_DR_UPI fill:#f3e5f5,stroke:#7b1fa2 style MGMT_DC_UPI fill:#fff3e0,stroke:#ef6c00 style MGMT_DR_UPI fill:#fff3e0,stroke:#ef6c00 ```

</details>

docs.title: UPI Install Flow Per Cluster
docs.text:
Each UPI cluster follows this phased install sequence. The bootstrap node is a temporary VM that is automatically cleaned up once the control plane becomes self-hosted:

Draw.io Source: UPI Install Flow

Download .drawio file — Open in draw.io for interactive editing.

View original Mermaid source

```mermaid

graph TB subgraph PHASE1["Phase 1: Prerequisites"] DNS["DNS Records"] LB["HAProxy LB ConfigBootstrap backend included"] QM["Quay Mirror(disconnected only)"] end

subgraph PHASE2["Phase 2: Ignition"]
    IC["install-config.yaml<br/><i>platform: none</i>"]
    IGN["openshift-install<br/>create ignition-configs"]
    HTTP["Bastion HTTP Server<br/><i>Serve *.ign on :8080</i>"]
end

subgraph PHASE3["Phase 3: Bootstrap + Control Plane"]
    BOOT["Boot Bootstrap VM<br/><i>Fetch bootstrap.ign</i>"]
    CP["Boot 3 Masters<br/><i>Fetch master.ign</i>"]
    BW["bootstrap_complete<br/><i>Control plane self-hosted</i>"]
    BC["bootstrap_cleanup<br/><i>Remove from HAProxy<br/>Power off VM</i>"]
end

subgraph PHASE4["Phase 4: Workers + CSRs"]
    WK["Boot Workers<br/><i>Fetch worker.ign</i>"]
    CSR["Approve CSRs"]
    CC["cluster_complete"]
end

subgraph PHASE5["Phase 5: Day-2 Operators"]
    D2["NFD, GPU, ODF, AI,<br/>ServiceMesh, Serverless,<br/>Submariner, ACM, ACS, Quay<br/><i>(per cluster role)</i>"]
end

PHASE1 --> PHASE2 --> PHASE3 --> PHASE4 --> PHASE5

style PHASE1 fill:#e3f2fd,stroke:#1565c0
style PHASE2 fill:#fff3e0,stroke:#ef6c00
style PHASE3 fill:#fce4ec,stroke:#c62828
style PHASE4 fill:#e8f5e9,stroke:#2e7d32
style PHASE5 fill:#f3e5f5,stroke:#7b1fa2
style BC fill:#ff8a80,stroke:#d50000,stroke-width:2px

```

docs.title: IPI vs UPI — Infrastructure Comparison
docs.text:
Component IPI UPI Bastion Host Optional (convenience) Required — runs openshift-install, serves ignition, PXE/TFTP Bootstrap VM Managed by installer, auto-removed Operator-provisioned — explicit bootstrap_cleanup module Load Balancer Installer-managed keepalived VIPs External HAProxy — must be pre-configured Node Boot Automated via BMC/iDRAC virtual media Manual: PXE, ISO, or operator-driven boot CSR Approval Automatic Explicit — oc adm certificate approve install-config platform: baremetal + BMC credentials platform: none — no hardware management Installer Binary openshift-baremetal-install openshift-install Node Definitions Include BMC address, user, password No BMC fields — only name, MAC, IP ## Cluster Roles & Responsibilities Cluster IPI Folder UPI Folder --------- ----------- ----------- DC Primary ipi-method/openshiftbaremetal/ upi-method/openshiftbaremetal/ DR Secondary ipi-method/openshiftbaremetal-dr/ upi-method/openshiftbaremetal-dr/ Management DC ipi-method/mgmt-dc/ upi-method/mgmt-dc/ Management DR ipi-method/mgmt-dr/ upi-method/mgmt-dr/ ## Network Architecture Each cluster uses non-overlapping CIDRs to support Submariner cross-cluster routing without Globalnet: Draw.io Source: Network Architecture

Download .drawio file — Open in draw.io for interactive editing.

View original Mermaid source

```mermaid

graph LR subgraph DC_NETS["DC Site Networks"] DC_MACHINE["Machine10.142.41.0/24"] DC_POD["Pod10.128.0.0/14"] DC_SVC["Service172.30.0.0/16"] MGMT_DC_MACHINE["Mgmt Machine10.142.42.0/24"] MGMT_DC_POD["Mgmt Pod10.136.0.0/14"] MGMT_DC_SVC["Mgmt Service172.28.0.0/16"] end subgraph DR_NETS["DR Site Networks"] DR_MACHINE["Machine10.143.41.0/24"] DR_POD["Pod10.132.0.0/14"] DR_SVC["Service172.31.0.0/16"] MGMT_DR_MACHINE["Mgmt Machine10.143.42.0/24"] MGMT_DR_POD["Mgmt Pod10.140.0.0/14"] MGMT_DR_SVC["Mgmt Service172.29.0.0/16"] end DC_NETS ↔|"L3 / WAN"| DR_NETS style DC_NETS fill:#e8f5e9,stroke:#2e7d32 style DR_NETS fill:#e3f2fd,stroke:#1565c0 ```

</details>

docs.title: 1. Deployment Flow — IPI
docs.text:
The IPI ADO pipeline deploys clusters in dependency order (installer manages node lifecycle via BMC):

Draw.io Source: Submariner Cross-Cluster Networking

Download .drawio file — Open in draw.io for interactive editing.

View original Mermaid source

```mermaid

sequenceDiagram participant ADO as Azure DevOps participant DC as DC Primary participant DR as DR Secondary participant MDC as Mgmt DC participant MDR as Mgmt DR

Note over ADO: User selects deployment scope

rect rgb(232, 245, 233)
    Note over ADO,DC: Stage 1 — DC Primary
    ADO->>DC: terraform init + apply
    DC-->>DC: DNS → Quay Mirror → OCP Install (BMC/iDRAC)
    DC-->>DC: NFD → GPU → ODF → AI Stack
    DC-->>DC: Submariner Broker (if enabled)
end

rect rgb(227, 242, 253)
    Note over ADO,DR: Stage 2 — DR Secondary
    ADO->>DR: terraform init + apply
    DR-->>DR: DNS → Quay Mirror → OCP Install (BMC/iDRAC)
    DR-->>DR: NFD → GPU → ODF → AI Stack
    DR-->>DR: Submariner Agent → connects to DC Broker
    DR-->>DR: ODF DR Replication (if enabled)
end

rect rgb(255, 243, 224)
    Note over ADO,MDC: Stage 3 — Management DC
    ADO->>MDC: terraform init + apply
    MDC-->>MDC: OCP Install → ODF
    MDC-->>MDC: ACM Hub → ACS Central → Quay
    MDC->>DC: Import cluster into ACM
    MDC->>DR: Import cluster into ACM
end

rect rgb(255, 243, 224)
    Note over ADO,MDR: Stage 4 — Management DR
    ADO->>MDR: terraform init + apply
    MDR-->>MDR: OCP Install → ODF
    MDR-->>MDR: ACM Standby → ACS Secured → Quay
end

```

docs.title: 1b. Deployment Flow — UPI
docs.text:
The UPI ADO pipeline adds bastion-driven ignition, manual/PXE/ISO node boot, and bootstrap cleanup phases:

Draw.io Source: ODF DR Replication

Download .drawio file — Open in draw.io for interactive editing.

View original Mermaid source

```mermaid

sequenceDiagram participant ADO as Azure DevOps participant BAS as Bastion Host participant DC as DC Primary participant DR as DR Secondary participant MDC as Mgmt DC participant MDR as Mgmt DR Note over ADO: User selects deployment scope + boot method rect rgb(232, 245, 233) Note over ADO,DC: Stage 1 — DC Primary (UPI) ADO->>BAS: Prerequisites: DNS + HAProxy + Quay Mirror ADO->>BAS: Generate ignition configs (platform: none) BAS->>BAS: Serve *.ign via HTTP :8080 BAS->>DC: Boot bootstrap VM → control plane nodes Note right of DC: PXE / ISO / Manual boot BAS->>BAS: wait-for bootstrap-complete BAS->>BAS: bootstrap_cleanup (remove VM + LB backend) BAS->>DC: Boot worker nodes → approve CSRs DC→>DC: NFD → GPU → ODF → AI → Submariner Broker end rect rgb(227, 242, 253) Note over ADO,DR: Stage 2 — DR Secondary (UPI) ADO->>BAS: Same UPI phases (ignition → boot → cleanup) DR→>DR: Submariner Agent → ODF DR Replication end rect rgb(255, 243, 224) Note over ADO,MDC: Stage 3 — Mgmt DC (UPI) ADO->>BAS: Same UPI phases MDC→>MDC: ACM Hub → ACS Central → Quay end rect rgb(255, 243, 224) Note over ADO,MDR: Stage 4 — Mgmt DR (UPI) ADO->>BAS: Same UPI phases MDR→>MDR: ACM Standby → ACS Secured → Quay end ```

</details>

docs.title: 2. Submariner Cross-Cluster Networking
docs.text:
Submariner establishes an encrypted IPsec tunnel between DC and DR for pod-to-pod and service-to-service communication:

Draw.io Source: Management Cluster Architecture

Download .drawio file — Open in draw.io for interactive editing.

View original Mermaid source

```mermaid

graph LR subgraph DC_CLUSTER["DC Primary"] GW_DC["Gateway Nodesubmariner.io/gateway=true"] POD_DC["AI Workload Pod10.128.x.x"] SVC_DC["ClusterIP Service"] end

subgraph DR_CLUSTER["DR Secondary"]
    GW_DR["Gateway Node<br/><i>submariner.io/gateway=true</i>"]
    POD_DR["AI Workload Pod<br/>10.132.x.x"]
    SVC_DR["ClusterIP Service"]
end

POD_DC --> SVC_DC
SVC_DC --> GW_DC
GW_DC <-->|"IPsec / Libreswan<br/>Cable Driver"| GW_DR
GW_DR --> SVC_DR
SVC_DR --> POD_DR

style DC_CLUSTER fill:#e8f5e9,stroke:#2e7d32
style DR_CLUSTER fill:#e3f2fd,stroke:#1565c0

`` </details> **How it works:** 1. DC Primary deploys Submariner as **broker** — creates theBrokerCR andSubmarinerCR 2. DR Secondary deploys Submariner as **agent** — connects to DC broker using API URL + token 3. Gateway nodes on each side establish IPsec tunnels using the configured cable driver (libreswan,wireguard, orvxlan) 4.ServiceDiscoveryenables DNS-based cross-cluster service resolution (..svc.clusterset.local`)

docs.title: 3. ODF Disaster Recovery Replication
docs.text:
ODF DR enables Ceph RBD mirroring between the DC and DR storage clusters:

Draw.io Source: ACM Import/DR Management

Download .drawio file — Open in draw.io for interactive editing.

View original Mermaid source

```mermaid

graph TB subgraph DC_STORAGE["DC Primary — ODF"] SC_DC["StorageClusterocs-storagecluster"] POOL_DC["CephBlockPoolmirroring: enabled"] PVC_DC["PVCs(AI Models, Datasets)"] end subgraph DR_STORAGE["DR Secondary — ODF"] SC_DR["StorageClusterocs-storagecluster"] POOL_DR["CephBlockPoolmirroring: enabled"] PVC_DR["PVCs(Replicated Data)"] end subgraph CONTROL["DR Control Plane"] ODR_OP["ODF DR Operator"] MIRROR_PEER["MirrorPeer CR"] S3_META["S3 Metadata Store"] end PVC_DC →|"Write"| POOL_DC POOL_DC ↔|"RBD Mirror Daemonasync: */5 * * * *sync: real-time"| POOL_DR POOL_DR → PVC_DR ODR_OP → MIRROR_PEER MIRROR_PEER → POOL_DC MIRROR_PEER → POOL_DR ODR_OP → S3_META style DC_STORAGE fill:#e8f5e9,stroke:#2e7d32 style DR_STORAGE fill:#e3f2fd,stroke:#1565c0 style CONTROL fill:#fff3e0,stroke:#ef6c00 ```

</details>
Mode Replication RPO Latency Requirement Use Case Regional DR (async) Scheduled (cron) Minutes Tolerant (cross-DC WAN) Multi-datacenter, 100+ km apart Metro DR (sync) Real-time Zero < 10ms RTT (metro-area) Same metro region, < 100 km

docs.title: 4. Management Cluster Architecture
docs.text:
The management clusters provide centralized operations across all workload clusters:

Draw.io Source: DR Failover Workflow

Download .drawio file — Open in draw.io for interactive editing.

View original Mermaid source

```mermaid

graph TB subgraph MGMT_DC_DETAIL["Management DC"] ACM_HUB2["ACM HubMultiClusterHub CR"] ACS_C["ACS CentralVulnerability ScanningPolicy EnforcementRisk Profiling"] QUAY_C["Quay EnterpriseImage RegistryClair ScanningMirror Sync"] OBS["ACM Observability(optional)"] end

subgraph MGMT_DR_DETAIL["Management DR"]
    ACM_S["<b>ACM Standby</b><br/>Passive Hub<br/><i>Promotable on failover</i>"]
    ACS_S["<b>ACS Secured</b><br/>Sensor + Collector<br/><i>Reports to DC Central</i>"]
    QUAY_S["<b>Quay Enterprise</b><br/>Geo-Replicated"]
end

subgraph WORKLOADS["Managed Workload Clusters"]
    W_DC["DC Primary"]
    W_DR["DR Secondary"]
end

ACM_HUB2 -->|"Cluster lifecycle<br/>Policy, Governance"| W_DC
ACM_HUB2 -->|"Cluster lifecycle<br/>Policy, Governance"| W_DR
ACS_C -->|"Security policies"| W_DC
ACS_C -->|"Security policies"| W_DR
ACS_C -->|"Central endpoint"| ACS_S
ACM_HUB2 -.->|"DR failover<br/>promotion"| ACM_S
QUAY_C -.->|"Geo-replication"| QUAY_S

style MGMT_DC_DETAIL fill:#fff3e0,stroke:#ef6c00
style MGMT_DR_DETAIL fill:#fff3e0,stroke:#ef6c00
style WORKLOADS fill:#f3e5f5,stroke:#7b1fa2

```

Management DC runs: - ACM Hub — imports and manages all clusters, deploys policies, handles governance - ACS Central — centralized security scanning, vulnerability management, compliance reporting - Quay Enterprise — on-cluster container registry with Clair image scanning Management DR runs: - ACM Standby — passive MultiClusterHub, promotable to active during DC failure - ACS SecuredCluster — sensor + collector only, reports back to ACS Central in DC - Quay Enterprise — geo-replicated registry for DR availability

docs.title: 4b. ACM Cluster Import & DR Application Management
docs.text:
In addition to the core management services, two dedicated modules and pipelines handle workload cluster import and application-level DR:

Draw.io Source: Inter-Cluster Connection Map

Download .drawio file — Open in draw.io for interactive editing.

View original Mermaid source

```mermaid

graph TB subgraph ACM_OPS["ACM Hub Operations"] IMPORT["ACM Cluster ImportManagedClusterKlusterletAddonConfigManagedClusterSet"] DR_OPS["ACM DR ApplicationsDRPolicyDRPlacementControlODR Hub Operator"] end subgraph PIPELINES["Dedicated Pipelines"] P_IMPORT["azure-pipelines-acm-import.ymlImport scope, feature toggles"] P_DR["azure-pipelines-acm-dr.ymlConfigure / Failover / Failback"] end subgraph TARGETS["Target Clusters"] DC_T["DC Primary"] DR_T["DR Secondary"] end P_IMPORT →|"terraform apply-var-file=acm-import.tfvars"| IMPORT P_DR →|"terraform apply-var-file=acm-dr.tfvars"| DR_OPS IMPORT →|"Import + Addons"| DC_T IMPORT →|"Import + Addons"| DR_T DR_OPS →|"Failover / Failback"| DC_T DR_OPS →|"Failover / Failback"| DR_T style ACM_OPS fill:#fff3e0,stroke:#ef6c00 style PIPELINES fill:#e8f5e9,stroke:#2e7d32 style TARGETS fill:#f3e5f5,stroke:#7b1fa2 ```

</details>
Capability Module Var File Pipeline Description Cluster Import acm-cluster-import acm-import.tfvars azure-pipelines-acm-import.yml Import workload clusters into ACM Hub DR Applications acm-dr-applications acm-dr.tfvars azure-pipelines-acm-dr.yml DRPolicy + failover/failback/relocate

Mirror Operators

The odr-hub-operator (stable-4.16) has been added to mirror_operators in all management cluster terraform.tfvars files to support disconnected ODR Hub deployment.

docs.title: 5. Disaster Recovery Failover Workflow
docs.text:
Draw.io Source: ADO Pipeline Scope

Download .drawio file — Open in draw.io for interactive editing.

View original Mermaid source

```mermaid

sequenceDiagram participant OP as Operations Team participant ADO as Azure DevOps participant MDC as Mgmt DC participant MDR as Mgmt DR participant DC as DC Primary participant DR as DR Secondary

Note over DC: ❌ DC Site Failure

OP->>ADO: Trigger DR failover pipeline
ADO->>MDR: Promote ACM Standby → Active Hub

rect rgb(255, 235, 238)
    Note over MDR,DR: Failover Actions
    MDR->>DR: Activate DR workloads
    MDR->>DR: Switch ODF MirrorPeer to primary
    DR-->>DR: PVCs become read-write
    DR-->>DR: AI workloads resume on DR
end

Note over DR: ✅ DR is now Active

rect rgb(232, 245, 233)
    Note over DC: DC Recovery
    OP->>ADO: Trigger DC recovery pipeline
    ADO->>DC: Rebuild / Restore DC cluster
    ADO->>DC: Re-enable ODF mirroring (reverse)
    ADO->>MDC: Restore ACM Hub as primary
end

```

docs.title: IPI Method
docs.text: ipi-method/ ├── openshiftbaremetal/ # DC Primary — workload cluster │ ├── main.tf │ ├── variables.tf │ ├── terraform.tfvars │ ├── outputs.tf │ ├── versions.tf │ └── modules/ # Shared modules (referenced by all clusters) │ ├── dns/ │ ├── haproxy/ │ ├── quay-mirror/ │ ├── ocp-baremetal/ │ ├── nfd-operator/ │ ├── gpu-operator/ │ ├── odf-operator/ │ ├── servicemesh/ │ ├── serverless/ │ ├── openshift-ai/ │ ├── gpu-monitoring/ │ ├── metallb-operator/ │ ├── sriov-operator/ │ ├── cluster-autoscaler/ │ ├── etcd-backup/ │ ├── submariner/ │ ├── odf-dr/ │ ├── acm/ │ ├── acs/ │ └── quay-enterprise/ │ ├── openshiftbaremetal-dr/ # DR Secondary — workload cluster │ ├── main.tf # References ../openshiftbaremetal/modules/ │ ├── variables.tf │ ├── terraform.tfvars │ ├── outputs.tf │ └── versions.tf │ ├── mgmt-dc/ # Management DC — ACM Hub + ACS Central + Quay │ ├── main.tf │ ├── variables.tf │ ├── terraform.tfvars │ ├── outputs.tf │ └── versions.tf │ ├── mgmt-dr/ # Management DR — ACM Standby + ACS Secured + Quay │ ├── main.tf │ ├── variables.tf │ ├── terraform.tfvars │ ├── outputs.tf │ └── versions.tf │ └── azure-pipelines.yml # ADO pipeline with deployment scope selection

docs.title: UPI Method
docs.text: upi-method/ ├── openshiftbaremetal/ # DC Primary — workload cluster (UPI) │ ├── main.tf # UPI phases: ignition → bootstrap → cleanup → workers │ ├── variables.tf # No BMC fields — boot_method, bootstrap_ip/mac │ ├── terraform.tfvars │ ├── outputs.tf │ ├── versions.tf │ └── modules/ # Shared UPI modules (referenced by all UPI clusters) │ ├── dns/ │ ├── haproxy/ # External HAProxy LB (required for UPI) │ ├── quay-mirror/ │ ├── install-config/ # platform: none │ ├── ignition/ # openshift-install create ignition-configs │ ├── ignition-server/ # HTTP server on bastion :8080 │ ├── bootstrap/ # Boot temporary bootstrap VM │ ├── control-plane/ # PXE/ISO/manual boot masters │ ├── bootstrap-complete/ # wait-for bootstrap-complete │ ├── bootstrap-cleanup/ # Remove bootstrap from LB + power off │ ├── compute-nodes/ # PXE/ISO/manual boot workers │ ├── cluster-complete/ # CSR approval + wait-for install-complete │ ├── nfd-operator/ │ ├── gpu-operator/ │ ├── odf-operator/ │ ├── servicemesh/ │ ├── serverless/ │ ├── openshift-ai/ │ ├── gpu-monitoring/ │ ├── metallb-operator/ │ ├── sriov-operator/ │ ├── cluster-autoscaler/ │ ├── etcd-backup/ │ ├── submariner/ │ ├── odf-dr/ │ ├── acm/ │ ├── acs/ │ └── quay-enterprise/ │ ├── openshiftbaremetal-dr/ # DR Secondary (UPI) │ ├── main.tf # References ../openshiftbaremetal/modules/ │ ├── variables.tf │ ├── terraform.tfvars │ ├── outputs.tf │ └── versions.tf │ ├── mgmt-dc/ # Management DC (UPI) │ ├── main.tf │ ├── variables.tf │ ├── terraform.tfvars │ ├── outputs.tf │ └── versions.tf │ ├── mgmt-dr/ # Management DR (UPI) │ ├── main.tf │ ├── variables.tf │ ├── terraform.tfvars │ ├── outputs.tf │ └── versions.tf │ └── azure-pipelines-upi.yml # ADO pipeline with UPI phases + deployment scope

docs.title: Key UPI Module Differences
docs.text:
IPI Module UPI Equivalent Difference ocp-baremetal/ install-config/ + ignition/ + ignition-server/ + bootstrap/ + control-plane/ + bootstrap-complete/ + bootstrap-cleanup/ + compute-nodes/ + cluster-complete/ Single IPI module → 9 UPI modules for phased install (none) bootstrap-cleanup/ New — removes temporary bootstrap VM after control plane is self-hosted haproxy/ (optional) haproxy/ (required) HAProxy is mandatory in UPI as there's no installer-managed keepalived ## ADO Pipeline — Deployment Scope Selection The Azure DevOps pipeline provides granular control over which clusters to deploy: Draw.io Source: IPI vs UPI Comparison

Download .drawio file — Open in draw.io for interactive editing.

View original Mermaid source

```mermaid

graph TD START["ADO Pipeline Trigger"] → SCOPE{"Deployment Scope?"} SCOPE →|dc-only| S1["Stage: DC Primary"] SCOPE →|dr-only| S2["Stage: DR Secondary"] SCOPE →|dc-and-dr| S1 → S2 SCOPE →|mgmt-dc-only| S3["Stage: Mgmt DC"] SCOPE →|mgmt-dr-only| S4["Stage: Mgmt DR"] SCOPE →|mgmt-clusters| S3 → S4 SCOPE →|all-dc| S1_2["DC Primary"] → S3_2["Mgmt DC"] SCOPE →|all-dr| S2_2["DR Secondary"] → S4_2["Mgmt DR"] SCOPE →|all| ALL["DC → DR → Mgmt DC → Mgmt DR"] OPTIONS["Optional Toggles"] OPTIONS → OPT1["☐ Enable Submariner"] OPTIONS → OPT2["☐ Enable ODF Replication"] OPTIONS → OPT3["ODF DR Mode:regional-dr / metro-dr"] OPTIONS → OPT4["Terraform Action:plan / apply / destroy"] style START fill:#1a237e,stroke:#1a237e,color:#fff style SCOPE fill:#ff8f00,stroke:#ff8f00,color:#fff ```

</details>
Parameter Values Description deploymentScope dc-only, dr-only, dc-and-dr, mgmt-dc-only, mgmt-dr-only, mgmt-clusters, all-dc, all-dr, all Which clusters to deploy enableSubmariner true / false Enable Submariner cross-cluster networking enableOdfReplication true / false Enable ODF storage replication odfDrMode regional-dr / metro-dr Async (RPO: minutes) or sync (RPO: zero) terraformAction plan / apply / destroy Terraform operation to run

docs.title: Step 1: Configure Each Cluster
docs.text:
Edit the terraform.tfvars in each folder:

# IPI Method
# DC Primary (workload)
vi ipi-method/openshiftbaremetal/terraform.tfvars

# DR Secondary (workload)
vi ipi-method/openshiftbaremetal-dr/terraform.tfvars

# Management DC (ACM + ACS + Quay)
vi ipi-method/mgmt-dc/terraform.tfvars

# Management DR (ACM standby + ACS secured + Quay)
vi ipi-method/mgmt-dr/terraform.tfvars

# UPI Method
vi upi-method/openshiftbaremetal/terraform.tfvars
vi upi-method/openshiftbaremetal-dr/terraform.tfvars
vi upi-method/mgmt-dc/terraform.tfvars
vi upi-method/mgmt-dr/terraform.tfvars

docs.title: Step 2: Deploy via ADO Pipeline
docs.text:
1. Navigate to Azure DevOps → Pipelines
  2. Select OpenShift Multi-Cluster Deployment
  3. Click Run pipeline

  4. Choose your parameters:

    Setting Recommended for Full Deploy Deployment Scope all Enable Submariner true Enable ODF Replication true ODF DR Mode regional-dr Terraform Action plan (review first, then apply)

docs.title: Step 3: Verify Deployment
docs.text:
# DC Primary
export KUBECONFIG=ipi-method/openshiftbaremetal/modules/ocp-baremetal/generated/kubeconfig
oc get nodes
oc get csv -A | grep -E "gpu|odf|rhods|submariner"

# DR Secondary
export KUBECONFIG=ipi-method/openshiftbaremetal-dr/modules/ocp-baremetal/generated/kubeconfig
oc get gateways.submariner.io -n submariner-operator    # Submariner status
oc get mirrorpeer -A                                     # ODF DR status

# Management DC
export KUBECONFIG=ipi-method/mgmt-dc/modules/ocp-baremetal/generated/kubeconfig
oc get multiclusterhub -n open-cluster-management       # ACM status
oc get central -n stackrox                               # ACS status
oc get quayregistry -n quay-enterprise                   # Quay status

docs.title: Complete Inter-Cluster Connection Map
docs.text:
Every connection between all four clusters at the protocol and port level:

📥 Download draw.io source [⬇ 13-protocol-port-map.drawio](../diagrams/architecture/13-protocol-port-map.drawio) 📝 Original Mermaid source 
graph TB
    subgraph DC_SITE["DC Site"]
        subgraph DC_WL["DC Primary Workload"]
            DC_API["API Server<br/>:6443"]
            DC_BROKER["Submariner Broker<br/>:8443"]
            DC_GW["Submariner Gateway<br/>:4500/UDP (IPsec)"]
            DC_ODF["ODF Ceph<br/>RBD Mirror Daemon"]
            DC_APPS["*.apps Route<br/>:443"]
        end
        subgraph MGMT_DC_C["Mgmt DC"]
            ACM_API["ACM Hub API<br/>:443"]
            ACS_CENT["ACS Central<br/>:443 gRPC"]
            QUAY_P["Quay Enterprise<br/>:443"]
            ACM_OBS["ACM Observability<br/>Thanos Receive :10902"]
        end
    end

    subgraph DR_SITE["DR Site"]
        subgraph DR_WL["DR Secondary Workload"]
            DR_API["API Server<br/>:6443"]
            DR_GW["Submariner Gateway<br/>:4500/UDP (IPsec)"]
            DR_ODF["ODF Ceph<br/>RBD Mirror Daemon"]
            DR_APPS["*.apps Route<br/>:443"]
        end
        subgraph MGMT_DR_C["Mgmt DR"]
            ACM_S["ACM Standby<br/>:443"]
            ACS_SC["ACS SecuredCluster<br/>Sensor :443"]
            QUAY_S["Quay Enterprise<br/>:443"]
        end
    end

    %% Submariner tunnel
    DC_GW <-->|"1 IPsec Tunnel<br/>UDP 4500 + 500<br/>ESP Protocol"| DR_GW

    %% ODF replication
    DC_ODF <-->|"2 RBD Mirroring<br/>TCP 6789, 3300<br/>Ceph Messenger v2"| DR_ODF

    %% ACM → managed clusters
    ACM_API -->|"3 Cluster Import<br/>Klusterlet Agent<br/>TCP 443"| DC_API
    ACM_API -->|"4 Cluster Import<br/>Klusterlet Agent<br/>TCP 443"| DR_API

    %% ACS Central → SecuredCluster
    ACS_SC -->|"5 gRPC Stream<br/>TCP 443"| ACS_CENT

    %% Quay geo-replication
    QUAY_P <-.->|"6 Geo-Replication<br/>TCP 443 (S3 API)"| QUAY_S

    %% ACM failover
    ACM_API -.->|"7 Failover<br/>Promotion"| ACM_S

    %% Submariner broker
    DR_GW -->|"8 Broker Registration<br/>TCP 6443"| DC_BROKER

    style DC_SITE fill:#e8f5e9,stroke:#2e7d32,stroke-width:2px
    style DR_SITE fill:#e3f2fd,stroke:#1565c0,stroke-width:2px
    style DC_WL fill:#c8e6c9,stroke:#388e3c
    style DR_WL fill:#bbdefb,stroke:#1976d2
    style MGMT_DC_C fill:#fff3e0,stroke:#ef6c00
    style MGMT_DR_C fill:#fff3e0,stroke:#ef6c00

docs.title: Connection Details
docs.text: # Source Destination Protocol / Port Purpose Direction 1 DC Gateway Node DR Gateway Node UDP 4500, 500 + ESP Submariner IPsec tunnel for pod-to-pod and service-to-service traffic Bidirectional 2 DC ODF Ceph DR ODF Ceph TCP 6789, 3300 Ceph RBD mirroring (async every 5 min or sync real-time) Bidirectional 3 ACM Hub (Mgmt DC) DC Primary API TCP 443 → 6443 Klusterlet agent import, policy delivery, observability metrics Hub → Managed 4 ACM Hub (Mgmt DC) DR Secondary API TCP 443 → 6443 Klusterlet agent import, policy delivery, observability metrics Hub → Managed 5 ACS SecuredCluster (Mgmt DR) ACS Central (Mgmt DC) TCP 443 (gRPC) Sensor telemetry, vulnerability reports, compliance results Sensor → Central 6 Quay Enterprise (Mgmt DC) Quay Enterprise (Mgmt DR) TCP 443 (S3 API) Image geo-replication via shared S3 backend Bidirectional 7 ACM Hub (Mgmt DC) ACM Standby (Mgmt DR) TCP 443 DR failover promotion (manual trigger) Failover 8 DR Submariner Agent DC Submariner Broker TCP 6443 Agent registration, tunnel negotiation, service discovery sync Agent → Broker

docs.title: Firewall Rules Required
docs.text: From To Ports Notes DC Gateway Nodes DR Gateway Nodes UDP 4500, 500 + ESP Submariner IPsec — both directions DC Ceph MON/OSD DR Ceph MON/OSD TCP 6789, 3300 RBD mirroring — both directions Mgmt DC DC Primary API VIP TCP 6443 ACM Klusterlet Mgmt DC DR Secondary API VIP TCP 6443 ACM Klusterlet Mgmt DR Mgmt DC ACS Route TCP 443 ACS Sensor → Central Mgmt DC Quay Mgmt DR Quay TCP 443 Geo-replication S3 sync DR Gateway Nodes DC API VIP TCP 6443 Submariner broker registration Local Quay (internet) cdn.redhat.com / quay.io TCP 443 Pull operators from Red Hat CDN Bastion Local Quay TCP 8443 oc mirror push and pull mirrored images Bastion Mgmt DC Quay Enterprise Route TCP 443 Replicate mirrored content to DC Quay Enterprise Bastion Mgmt DR Quay Enterprise Route TCP 443 Replicate mirrored content to DR Quay Enterprise All Cluster Nodes Local Quay / Quay Enterprise TCP 443/8443 Pull container images All Nodes DNS Server TCP/UDP 53 DNS resolution All Nodes NTP Server UDP 123 Time synchronization Bastion All BMC (iDRAC/iLO/XCC) TCP 443 Redfish API bare metal provisioning Submariner Gateway DC Submariner Gateway DR UDP 4800 VXLAN tunnel

docs.title: Terraform Automation — DR Secondary Workload Cluster
docs.text:
The DR Secondary cluster mirrors the DC Primary workload cluster with Submariner agent connectivity and ODF DR replication support.

Multi-Cluster Architecture

This is one of four cluster environments. See also: Multi-Cluster Overview | DC Primary | Management DC | Management DR

docs.title: Architecture
docs.text:
Draw.io Source: DR Secondary Architecture

Download .drawio file — Open in draw.io for interactive editing.

View original Mermaid source

```mermaid

graph LR subgraph DR_CLUSTER["DR Secondary Workload Cluster"] direction TB A[DNS Validation] → B[Quay Mirror] B → C[HAProxy LB] C → D[OCP Baremetal Install] D → E[NFD Operator] E → F[GPU Operator] D → G[ODF Operator] D → H[Service Mesh] D → I[Serverless] D → N[MetalLB] D → O[SR-IOV] F & G & H & I → J[OpenShift AI] F → K[GPU Monitoring] D → L[Cluster Autoscaler] D → M[etcd Backup] D → P["SubmarinerAgent"] G & P → Q[ODF DR Replication] end

subgraph DC["DC Primary"]
    BROKER["Submariner<br/><b>Broker</b>"]
    ODF_DC["ODF Storage"]
end

P <-->|"IPsec Tunnel"| BROKER
Q <-->|"RBD Mirroring"| ODF_DC

style DR_CLUSTER fill:#e3f2fd,stroke:#1565c0,stroke-width:2px
style DC fill:#e8f5e9,stroke:#2e7d32

```

docs.title: What's Different from DC Primary
docs.text:
Aspect DC Primary DR Secondary Folder ipi-method/openshiftbaremetal/ ipi-method/openshiftbaremetal-dr/ Modules Local (./modules/) Referenced (../openshiftbaremetal/modules/) Submariner role Broker Agent (connects to DC broker) ODF DR Source Target (receives replicated data) Pod CIDR 10.128.0.0/14 10.132.0.0/14 (non-overlapping) Service CIDR 172.30.0.0/16 172.31.0.0/16 (non-overlapping) Machine CIDR 10.142.41.0/24 10.143.41.0/24 (DR site) ## Submariner Agent Configuration The DR cluster connects to the DC Primary broker: Draw.io Source: DR Secondary Connectivity

Download .drawio file — Open in draw.io for interactive editing.

View original Mermaid source

```mermaid

sequenceDiagram participant DC as DC Primary (Broker) participant DR as DR Secondary (Agent) DC->>DC: Deploy Submariner Operator DC->>DC: Create Broker CR DC->>DC: Deploy Submariner CR (broker mode) Note over DC: Broker ready, listening DR->>DR: Deploy Submariner Operator DR->>DC: Connect using broker_api_url + token DR->>DR: Deploy Submariner CR (agent mode) DC↔DR: IPsec tunnel established DC↔DR: Service discovery active Note over DC,DR: Pods can reach services across clustersvia *.svc.clusterset.local ```

</details>

# terraform.tfvars (DR Secondary)
enable_submariner              = true
submariner_broker_api_url      = "https://api.ocp-ai.example.com:6443"
submariner_broker_token        = "REPLACE_BROKER_TOKEN"
submariner_cable_driver        = "libreswan"
submariner_globalnet_enabled   = false

docs.title: ODF DR Replication
docs.text:
Once both Submariner and ODF are enabled, ODF DR can replicate PVCs:

# terraform.tfvars (DR Secondary)
enable_odf_dr               = true
odf_dr_mode                 = "regional-dr"     # async
odf_dr_replication_schedule = "*/5 * * * *"      # every 5 minutes
odf_dr_peer_cluster_name    = "ocp-ai"           # DC Primary cluster name
odf_dr_s3_endpoint          = "https://rgw.ocp-ai.example.com"
odf_dr_s3_bucket            = "odf-dr-metadata"
odf_dr_s3_access_key        = "REPLACE_S3_ACCESS_KEY"
odf_dr_s3_secret_key        = "REPLACE_S3_SECRET_KEY"

docs.title: Variable Reference — DR-Specific
docs.text: All DC Primary variables are also available. The following are unique to DR:

docs.title: Submariner Agent
docs.text: Variable Type Default Description enable_submariner bool false Enable Submariner agent submariner_broker_api_url string — DC Primary API URL submariner_broker_token string — Broker SA token submariner_broker_ca string — Broker cluster CA submariner_cable_driver string libreswan Tunnel driver submariner_globalnet_enabled bool false Globalnet for overlapping CIDRs

docs.title: ODF DR
docs.text: Variable Type Default Description enable_odf_dr bool false Enable ODF DR replication odf_dr_mode string regional-dr regional-dr or metro-dr odf_dr_replication_schedule string */5 * * * * Async mirror schedule odf_dr_peer_cluster_name string — DC Primary cluster name odf_dr_s3_endpoint string — S3 endpoint for metadata odf_dr_s3_bucket string odf-dr-metadata S3 bucket

docs.title: Cross-Cluster Connectivity — DR Secondary
docs.text:
The DR Secondary has connections to DC Primary (Submariner + ODF) and both management clusters:

Draw.io Source: DR Failover ACM Promotion

Download .drawio file — Open in draw.io for interactive editing.

View original Mermaid source

```mermaid

graph TB subgraph DR_SECONDARY["DR Secondary Workload Cluster"] OCP_DR["OCP 4.15 Baremetal"] SUB_AGENT["SubmarinerAgent + Gateway:4500 UDP"] ODF_DR_CEPH["ODF Ceph StorageRBD Mirror (Target):6789 / :3300"] RHOAI_DR["OpenShift AIModel Serving"] GPU_DR["NVIDIA GPU Stack"] end

subgraph DC_PRIMARY["DC Primary"]
    DC_BROKER["Submariner<br/><b>Broker</b><br/>:6443 / :8443"]
    DC_GW["Submariner<br/><b>Gateway</b>"]
    DC_ODF["ODF Ceph<br/>RBD Mirror (Source)"]
end

subgraph MGMT_DC_BOX["Mgmt DC"]
    ACM["ACM Hub<br/>Klusterlet"]
    ACS["ACS Central<br/>Sensor"]
end

subgraph MGMT_DR_BOX["Mgmt DR"]
    ACM_DR["ACM Standby<br/>(Failover Hub)"]
end

%% Submariner
SUB_AGENT -->|"1 Broker Registration<br/>TCP 6443"| DC_BROKER
SUB_AGENT <-->|"2 IPsec Tunnel<br/>UDP 4500 + ESP"| DC_GW

%% ODF replication
DC_ODF -->|"3 RBD Writes Replicated<br/>TCP 6789, 3300"| ODF_DR_CEPH

%% ACM management
ACM -->|"4 Klusterlet Import<br/>TCP 6443"| OCP_DR

%% ACS scanning
ACS -->|"5 SecuredCluster<br/>Sensor deployed"| OCP_DR

%% DR failover path
ACM_DR -.->|"6 Failover: re-import<br/>as managed cluster"| OCP_DR

style DR_SECONDARY fill:#e3f2fd,stroke:#1565c0,stroke-width:2px
style DC_PRIMARY fill:#e8f5e9,stroke:#2e7d32
style MGMT_DC_BOX fill:#fff3e0,stroke:#ef6c00
style MGMT_DR_BOX fill:#fff3e0,stroke:#ef6c00

```

| # | Connection | Direction | Protocol | Purpose | |---|------------|-----------|----------|----------| | 1 | DR Agent → DC Broker | Outbound | TCP 6443 | Register with Submariner broker | | 2 | DR Gateway ↔ DC Gateway | Bidirectional | UDP 4500 + ESP | Encrypted pod-to-pod tunnel | | 3 | DC Ceph → DR Ceph | Inbound | TCP 6789, 3300 | Receive replicated RBD images | | 4 | ACM Hub → DR API | Inbound | TCP 6443 | Klusterlet agent, governance policies | | 5 | ACS Central → DR | Inbound | TCP 443 | SecuredCluster sensor deployment | | 6 | ACM Standby → DR | Failover | TCP 6443 | Re-import on DC site failure |

docs.title: LDAP / OAuth Integration
docs.text:
The LDAP/OAuth module configures OpenShift to authenticate users against a corporate LDAP directory. It deploys: 1. OAuth CR — Adds an LDAP identity provider to the cluster OAuth configuration 2. LDAP Group Sync CronJob — Periodically syncs LDAP groups into OpenShift Group objects 3. RBAC ClusterRoleBindings — Maps LDAP groups to OpenShift ClusterRoles 4. Optional kubeadmin removal — Removes the default kubeadmin user after LDAP is configured | Variable | Type | Default | Description | |----------|------|---------|-------------| | enable_ldap | bool | false | Enable LDAP identity provider | | ldap_url | string | "" | LDAP URL (ldaps://host:636/...) | | ldap_bind_dn | string | "" | Service account DN | | ldap_bind_password | string | "" | Bind password (sensitive) | | enable_ldap_group_sync | bool | true | Enable group sync CronJob | | ldap_group_role_bindings | list(object) | [] | Group-to-ClusterRole mappings | | disable_kubeadmin | bool | false | Remove kubeadmin (irreversible) |

Mirrored Operators (Updated)

The DR Secondary cluster now also mirrors Submariner (stable-0.18), OADP (stable-1.4), and ODR (stable-4.16) operators.

docs.title: Day 1 vs Day 2 Separation
docs.text:
Phase Var File Pipeline Scope Day 1 — Install terraform.tfvars azure-pipelines.yml Cluster install, core operators, networking Day 2 — Post-Install day2-terraform.tfvars azure-pipelines-day2.yml Logging, OADP backup, LDAP/OAuth, GitOps (ArgoCD), Pipelines (Tekton) Day 2 operations are applied separately after cluster installation completes: ```bash terraform apply -var-file=terraform.tfvars -var-file=day2-terraform.tfvars ```

See the Day 2 Pipeline documentation for details.

docs.title: Quick Start
docs.text:
cd openshiftbaremetal-dr/
vi terraform.tfvars        # Update DR site values
terraform init
terraform plan
terraform apply

docs.title: Terraform Automation — Management Cluster DC
docs.text:
The Management DC cluster is the central operations hub running ACM, ACS Central, and Quay Enterprise to manage all workload clusters.

Multi-Cluster Architecture

See also: Multi-Cluster Overview | DC Primary | DR Secondary | Management DR

docs.title: Architecture
docs.text:
Draw.io Source: Mgmt DC Architecture

Download .drawio file — Open in draw.io for interactive editing.

View original Mermaid source

```mermaid

graph TB subgraph MGMT_DC["Management Cluster — DC"] direction TB A[DNS] → B[Quay Mirror] B → C[HAProxy LB] C → D[OCP Baremetal Install] D → G[ODF Storage] D → LB[MetalLB] G → ACM["ACM HubMultiClusterHub"] G → ACS["ACS CentralVulnerability Scanning"] G → QUAY["Quay EnterpriseContainer Registry"] D → ETCD[etcd Backup] end

subgraph MANAGED["Managed Clusters"]
    DC["DC Primary<br/>(Workload)"]
    DR["DR Secondary<br/>(Workload)"]
    MDR["Mgmt DR<br/>(Standby)"]
end

ACM -->|"Import & Manage"| DC
ACM -->|"Import & Manage"| DR
ACM -.->|"Failover"| MDR
ACS -->|"Security Policies"| DC
ACS -->|"Security Policies"| DR

style MGMT_DC fill:#fff3e0,stroke:#ef6c00,stroke-width:2px
style MANAGED fill:#f3e5f5,stroke:#7b1fa2

```

# Connection Direction Protocol Purpose 1-2 ACM Hub → DC/DR API Outbound TCP 6443 Deploy Klusterlet, deliver policies, governance 3-4 DC/DR Klusterlet → ACM Hub Inbound TCP 443 Cluster status, health, resource inventory 5-6 DC/DR Metrics → Observability Inbound TCP 10902 Prometheus remote-write to Thanos Receive 7-9 Sensors → ACS Central Inbound TCP 443 (gRPC) CVE reports, compliance results, runtime alerts 10 Quay DC ↔ Quay DR Bidirectional TCP 443 (S3) Image geo-replication via shared S3 storage 11 ACM Hub → ACM Standby Failover TCP 443 Manual failover triggers DR promotion

DEV->>QDC: docker push image:tag
QDC->>QDC: Clair vulnerability scan

docs.title: Quay Mirror Replication (Local Quay → Quay Enterprise)
docs.text:
The quay-mirror-replicate module replicates operator and OCP release images from the internet-facing local Quay to the on-cluster Quay Enterprise instance.

Draw.io Source: Quay Geo-Replication Flow

Download .drawio file — Open in draw.io for interactive editing.

View original Mermaid source

```mermaid

graph LR CDN["Red Hat CDNcdn.redhat.com / quay.io"] →|"oc mirror"| LQ["Local Quay(Internet-Facing):8443"] LQ →|"skopeo copy"| QE_DC["Quay EnterpriseMgmt DC:443"] LQ →|"skopeo copy"| QE_DR["Quay EnterpriseMgmt DR:443"] QE_DC →|"Image Pull"| WC1["DC Workload Cluster"] QE_DR →|"Image Pull"| WC2["DR Workload Cluster"]

style CDN fill:#ef6c00,color:#fff
style LQ fill:#1565c0,color:#fff
style QE_DC fill:#2e7d32,color:#fff
style QE_DR fill:#2e7d32,color:#fff

`` </details> | Variable | Type | Default | Description | |----------|------|---------|-------------| |enable_quay_mirror_replicate| bool |false| Enable replication from local Quay to Quay Enterprise | |quay_enterprise_route| string |""| Quay Enterprise route URL (auto-discovered if empty) | |quay_enterprise_password| string |""| Quay Enterprise admin password | |quay_enterprise_mirror_org| string |ocp4-mirror` | Organization in Quay Enterprise for mirrored content |

Operator Mirroring Flow

  1. Local Quay (internet-facing) downloads operators from Red Hat CDN using oc mirror
  2. quay-mirror-replicate module replicates all images from local Quay to Quay Enterprise on the management cluster
  3. An ImageDigestMirrorSet is applied to redirect cluster image pulls to the local Quay Enterprise
  4. A CatalogSource pointing to the replicated operator index is created in the cluster

docs.title: LDAP / OAuth Integration
docs.text:
The LDAP/OAuth module configures OpenShift to authenticate users against a corporate LDAP directory. It deploys: 1. OAuth CR — Adds an LDAP identity provider to the cluster OAuth configuration 2. LDAP Group Sync CronJob — Periodically syncs LDAP groups into OpenShift Group objects 3. RBAC ClusterRoleBindings — Maps LDAP groups to OpenShift ClusterRoles 4. Optional kubeadmin removal — Removes the default kubeadmin user after LDAP is configured | Variable | Type | Default | Description | |----------|------|---------|-------------| | enable_ldap | bool | false | Enable LDAP identity provider | | ldap_url | string | "" | LDAP URL (ldaps://host:636/...) | | ldap_bind_dn | string | "" | Service account DN | | ldap_bind_password | string | "" | Bind password (sensitive) | | enable_ldap_group_sync | bool | true | Enable group sync CronJob | | ldap_group_role_bindings | list(object) | [] | Group-to-ClusterRole mappings | | disable_kubeadmin | bool | false | Remove kubeadmin (irreversible) |

Mirrored Operators (Updated)

The Management DC cluster now also mirrors OADP (stable-1.4), Submariner (stable-0.18), and ODR Hub operators.

docs.title: ACM Cluster Import
docs.text:
The Management DC cluster can import workload clusters (DC Primary, DR Secondary) into the ACM Hub as ManagedClusters. This enables centralized governance, policy delivery, and observability across all clusters.

📥 Download draw.io source ⬇ 23-acm-import-topology.drawio 📝 Original Mermaid source mermaid graph LR HUB["ACM Hub<br/>(Mgmt DC)"] -->|"Import"| DC["DC Primary<br/>ManagedCluster"] HUB -->|"Import"| DR["DR Secondary<br/>ManagedCluster"] HUB --> SET["ManagedClusterSet<br/>ocp-workload-clusters"] SET -.-> DC SET -.-> DR style HUB fill:#fff3e0,stroke:#ef6c00,stroke-width:2px style DC fill:#e8f5e9,stroke:#2e7d32 style DR fill:#e3f2fd,stroke:#1565c0

Resource Description ManagedCluster Registers each workload cluster with ACM and deploys the Klusterlet agent KlusterletAddonConfig Enables add-ons: Application Manager, Policy Controller, Search, Cert Policy, IAM Policy ManagedClusterSet Groups clusters for placement and policy scoping

Dedicated Pipeline

ACM Cluster Import has a separate pipeline: ACM Cluster Import Pipeline 

terraform apply -var-file=terraform.tfvars -var-file=acm-import.tfvars

docs.title: ACM DR Applications
docs.text:
The Management DC cluster manages application-level disaster recovery using OpenShift DR (ODR). DRPolicy and DRPlacementControl resources are created on the ACM Hub to protect applications across DC and DR clusters.

📥 Download draw.io source [⬇ 24-acm-dr-application-failover.drawio](../diagrams/clusters/24-acm-dr-application-failover.drawio) 📝 Original Mermaid source 
graph TB
    HUB["ACM Hub<br/>(Mgmt DC)"] --> DRP["DRPolicy<br/>dc-dr-policy"]
    DRP --> DC_CL["DRCluster<br/>dc-primary"]
    DRP --> DR_CL["DRCluster<br/>dr-secondary"]
    HUB --> DRPC["DRPlacementControl<br/>per-application"]
    DRPC -->|"Normal: Active"| DC_CL
    DRPC -->|"Failover: Promote"| DR_CL

    style HUB fill:#fff3e0,stroke:#ef6c00,stroke-width:2px
    style DRP fill:#e8f5e9,stroke:#2e7d32
    style DC_CL fill:#e8f5e9,stroke:#2e7d32
    style DR_CL fill:#e3f2fd,stroke:#1565c0
Action Description Configure Create DRPolicy + DRPlacementControl (initial setup) Failover Move applications to DR cluster when DC is unavailable Failback Restore applications to DC after recovery Relocate Planned migration between healthy clusters

Dedicated Pipeline

ACM DR has a separate pipeline: ACM DR Failover/Failback Pipeline 

terraform apply -var-file=terraform.tfvars -var-file=acm-dr.tfvars

docs.title: Day 1 vs Day 2 Separation
docs.text:
Phase Var File Pipeline Scope Day 1 — Install terraform.tfvars azure-pipelines.yml Cluster install, core operators, networking Day 2 — Post-Install day2-terraform.tfvars azure-pipelines-day2.yml Logging, OADP backup, LDAP/OAuth, GitOps (ArgoCD), Pipelines (Tekton)

Day 2 operations are applied separately after cluster installation completes:

terraform apply -var-file=terraform.tfvars -var-file=day2-terraform.tfvars

See the Day 2 Pipeline documentation for details.

docs.title: Terraform Automation — Management Cluster DR
docs.text:
The Management DR cluster is the standby operations hub that can be promoted to active during a DC site failure.

Multi-Cluster Architecture

See also: Multi-Cluster Overview | DC Primary | DR Secondary | Management DC

docs.text:
The Management DR cluster connects inbound to Mgmt DC services and stands ready for failover promotion:

Draw.io Source: Mgmt DR Connectivity

Download .drawio file — Open in draw.io for interactive editing.

View original Mermaid source

```mermaid

graph TB subgraph MGMT_DR_FULL["Mgmt DR — Standby Operations Hub"] ACM_STANDBY["ACM StandbyPassive MultiClusterHub"] ACS_SENSOR["ACS SecuredClusterSensor + Collector + Admission"] QUAY_REPLICA["Quay EnterpriseGeo-Replicated Registry"] end subgraph MGMT_DC_BOX["Mgmt DC (Active Hub)"] ACM_ACTIVE["ACM HubActive MultiClusterHub"] ACS_CENTRAL_DC["ACS Central:443 gRPC endpoint"] QUAY_PRIMARY["Quay EnterprisePrimary Registry"] end subgraph WORKLOADS["Workload Clusters"] DC_CL["DC Primary"] DR_CL["DR Secondary"] end %% ACS sensor -> central ACS_SENSOR →|"1 gRPC Sensor StreamTCP 443CVEs, compliance, runtime"| ACS_CENTRAL_DC %% Quay replication QUAY_PRIMARY <-.->|"2 Geo-ReplicationS3 API TCP 443Image layers + manifests"| QUAY_REPLICA %% ACM failover ACM_ACTIVE -.->|"3 Failover Promotion(manual trigger)"| ACM_STANDBY %% Post-failover connections ACM_STANDBY -.->|"4 After failover:Import + manage"| DC_CL ACM_STANDBY -.->|"5 After failover:Import + manage"| DR_CL style MGMT_DR_FULL fill:#fff3e0,stroke:#ef6c00,stroke-width:2px style MGMT_DC_BOX fill:#e8f5e9,stroke:#2e7d32 style WORKLOADS fill:#f3e5f5,stroke:#7b1fa2 ```

# Connection Direction Protocol Purpose 1 ACS Sensor → ACS Central (DC) Outbound TCP 443 (gRPC) Vulnerability reports, compliance results, runtime alerts 2 Quay DC ↔ Quay DR Bidirectional TCP 443 (S3 API) Image geo-replication via shared S3 backend 3 ACM Hub → ACM Standby Inbound (failover) TCP 443 Promotion trigger during DC site failure 4-5 ACM Standby → Workloads Post-failover TCP 6443 Re-import managed clusters after promotion

docs.text: Component Mgmt DC Mgmt DR ACM Active Hub — manages all clusters Standby — promotable on failover ACS Central — full UI, policies, scanner SecuredCluster only — sensor + collector Quay Primary registry Geo-replicated mirror ODF Full storage cluster Full storage cluster

docs.title: DR Failover — ACM Promotion
docs.text:
Draw.io Source: ACM DR Applications

Download .drawio file — Open in draw.io for interactive editing.

View original Mermaid source

```mermaid

sequenceDiagram participant OP as Operations Team participant DC as Mgmt DC (down) participant DR as Mgmt DR participant WL_DC as DC Primary Workload participant WL_DR as DR Secondary Workload

Note over DC: ❌ DC site failure detected

rect rgb(255, 235, 238)
    Note over OP,DR: Phase 1 — ACM Failover
    OP->>DR: oc patch multiclusterhub<br/>--type=merge -p '{"spec":{"disableHubSelfManagement":false}}'
    DR->>DR: ACM Standby → Active Hub
    DR->>DR: Start MultiClusterHub controllers
end

rect rgb(227, 242, 253)
    Note over DR,WL_DR: Phase 2 — Re-Import Clusters
    DR->>WL_DR: Import DR workload cluster (Klusterlet)
    WL_DR-->>DR: Cluster status: Ready
    Note over WL_DC: DC workload may still<br/>be reachable if only<br/>Mgmt DC is down
    DR->>WL_DC: Re-import DC workload (if reachable)
end

rect rgb(232, 245, 233)
    Note over DR: Phase 3 — Restore Services
    DR->>DR: ACS Sensor continues reporting locally
    DR->>DR: Quay serves images from geo-replica
    DR->>DR: Re-apply governance policies to managed clusters
end

Note over DR: ✅ Mgmt DR is now the active ACM Hub
Note over DR: All governance, security,<br/>and registry operations<br/>continue from DR site

```

docs.title: Quay Geo-Replication — Failover Behavior
docs.text: 📥 Download draw.io source ⬇ 25-quay-failover-behavior.drawio 📝 Original Mermaid source mermaid graph TB subgraph NORMAL["Normal Operation"] DEV1["CI/CD Pipeline"] -->|push| QDC1["Quay DC - Primary"] QDC1 -->|geo-replicate| QDR1["Quay DR - Replica"] NODE1["DR Workers"] -->|pull from local| QDR1 end subgraph FAILOVER["After DC Failure"] DEV2["CI/CD Pipeline"] -->|push| QDR2["Quay DR - Promoted"] QDR2 -->|images served locally| NODE2["All Workers"] end NORMAL -.->|DC Failure| FAILOVER style NORMAL fill:#e8f5e9,stroke:#2e7d32 style FAILOVER fill:#fff3e0,stroke:#ef6c00

docs.title: Variable Reference — DR-Specific
docs.text:
Variable Type Default Description enable_acm bool true Deploy ACM (standby mode) enable_acs bool true Deploy ACS SecuredCluster (no Central) acs_central_endpoint string — ACS Central endpoint from Mgmt DC enable_quay_enterprise bool true Deploy Quay (geo-replicated)

All other variables are identical to Management DC.

docs.title: Quay Mirror Replication (Local Quay → Quay Enterprise)
docs.text:
The quay-mirror-replicate module replicates operator and OCP release images from the internet-facing local Quay to the on-cluster Quay Enterprise instance on the DR management cluster.

Variable Type Default Description enable_quay_mirror_replicate bool false Enable replication from local Quay to Quay Enterprise quay_enterprise_route string "" Quay Enterprise route URL (auto-discovered if empty) quay_enterprise_password string "" Quay Enterprise admin password quay_enterprise_mirror_org string ocp4-mirror Organization in Quay Enterprise for mirrored content

Operator Mirroring Flow

The DR cluster receives the same mirrored operator content as the DC cluster. The local Quay (internet-facing) downloads from Red Hat CDN, then the replicate module pushes to both DC and DR Quay Enterprise instances independently.

docs.title: LDAP / OAuth Integration
docs.text:
The LDAP/OAuth module configures OpenShift to authenticate users against a corporate LDAP directory. It deploys:

  1. OAuth CR — Adds an LDAP identity provider to the cluster OAuth configuration
  2. LDAP Group Sync CronJob — Periodically syncs LDAP groups into OpenShift Group objects
  3. RBAC ClusterRoleBindings — Maps LDAP groups to OpenShift ClusterRoles
  4. Optional kubeadmin removal — Removes the default kubeadmin user after LDAP is configured
Variable Type Default Description enable_ldap bool false Enable LDAP identity provider ldap_url string "" LDAP URL (ldaps://host:636/...) ldap_bind_dn string "" Service account DN ldap_bind_password string "" Bind password (sensitive) enable_ldap_group_sync bool true Enable group sync CronJob ldap_group_role_bindings list(object) [] Group-to-ClusterRole mappings disable_kubeadmin bool false Remove kubeadmin (irreversible)

Mirrored Operators (Updated)

The Management DR cluster now also mirrors OADP (stable-1.4), Submariner (stable-0.18), and ODR Hub operators.

docs.title: ACM Cluster Import (Post-Failover)
docs.text:
After failover promotion, the Management DR cluster can import workload clusters into its ACM Hub. The same acm-import.tfvars configuration and pipeline are used as on Mgmt DC.

📥 Download draw.io source [⬇ 26-acm-post-failover-reimport.drawio](../diagrams/clusters/26-acm-post-failover-reimport.drawio) 📝 Original Mermaid source 
graph LR
    HUB["ACM Hub<br/>(Mgmt DR — Promoted)"] -->|"Re-Import"| DC["DC Primary<br/>ManagedCluster"]
    HUB -->|"Re-Import"| DR["DR Secondary<br/>ManagedCluster"]

    style HUB fill:#fff3e0,stroke:#ef6c00,stroke-width:2px
    style DC fill:#e8f5e9,stroke:#2e7d32
    style DR fill:#e3f2fd,stroke:#1565c0

Dedicated Pipeline

ACM Cluster Import: ACM Cluster Import Pipeline Set acmHub = mgmt-dr to target the promoted DR hub.

docs.title: ACM DR Applications (Post-Failover)
docs.text:
After promotion, the Management DR cluster takes over DR application management. DRPolicy and DRPlacementControl resources are re-created to manage failback to the recovered DC site.

Dedicated Pipeline

ACM DR: ACM DR Failover/Failback Pipeline Set acmHub = mgmt-dr when running from the promoted DR hub.

Day 2 operations are applied separately after cluster installation completes:

See the Day 2 Pipeline documentation for details.

docs.title: Terraform Automation — DC Primary Workload Cluster
docs.text:
This section provides the Terraform IaC for the DC Primary workload cluster — OpenShift Container Platform on bare metal with Red Hat OpenShift AI, NVIDIA GPU support, and all required operators.

Multi-Cluster Architecture

This is one of four cluster environments. See also: DR Secondary | Management DC | Management DR | ADO Pipeline

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
The Terraform project automates the full deployment lifecycle:

Draw.io Source: DC Primary Deployment Overview

Download .drawio file — Open in draw.io for interactive editing.

View original Mermaid source

```mermaid

graph LR A[DNS Validation] → B[Quay Mirror Registry] B → C[HAProxy LB] C → D[OCP Baremetal Install] D → E[NFD Operator] E → F[GPU Operator] D → G[ODF Operator] D → H[Service Mesh] D → I[Serverless] D → N[MetalLB] D → O[SR-IOV] F & G & H & I → J[OpenShift AI] F → K[GPU Monitoring] D → L[Cluster Autoscaler] D → M[etcd Backup] D → P[Submariner Broker] G & P → Q[ODF DR Replication] 

    </details>


## Project Structure
ipi-method/openshiftbaremetal/ ├── main.tf # Main orchestration — module wiring & dependencies ├── variables.tf # All variable declarations with types & defaults ├── terraform.tfvars # Your environment-specific values (edit this) ├── outputs.tf # Cluster URLs, kubeconfig path ├── versions.tf # Provider version constraints └── modules/ ├── dns/ # DNS record generation & validation ├── haproxy/ # HAProxy load balancer deployment ├── quay-mirror/ # Local Quay mirror registry (disconnected install) ├── ocp-baremetal/ # OCP install-config.yaml generation & installer ├── nfd-operator/ # Node Feature Discovery operator ├── gpu-operator/ # NVIDIA GPU Operator + ClusterPolicy ├── odf-operator/ # OpenShift Data Foundation (Ceph storage) ├── servicemesh/ # OpenShift Service Mesh + Kiali ├── serverless/ # OpenShift Serverless (KnativeServing) ├── openshift-ai/ # Red Hat OpenShift AI + DataScienceCluster ├── gpu-monitoring/ # NVIDIA DCGM Exporter dashboard ├── metallb-operator/ # MetalLB load balancer (optional) ├── sriov-operator/ # SR-IOV Network Operator (optional) ├── cluster-autoscaler/ # GPU-aware Cluster Autoscaler ├── etcd-backup/ # Nightly etcd backup CronJob ├── submariner/ # Submariner cross-cluster networking (DC↔DR) ├── odf-dr/ # ODF DR replication (async/sync mirroring) ├── acm/ # Red Hat ACM (management clusters) ├── acs/ # Red Hat ACS/StackRox (management clusters) ├── quay-enterprise/ # Red Hat Quay on-cluster registry (management clusters) ├── ldap-oauth/ # LDAP/OAuth identity provider + group sync ├── cluster-logging/ # Cluster Logging with ElasticSearch/Fluentd ├── oadp/ # OADP Backup & Recovery ├── openshift-gitops/ # ArgoCD GitOps operator ├── openshift-pipelines/ # Tekton CI/CD Pipelines ├── acm-cluster-import/ # ACM managed cluster import (mgmt clusters) ├── acm-dr-applications/ # ACM DR application failover (mgmt clusters) ├── compliance-operator/ # OpenSCAP compliance scanning (CIS/NIST/PCI-DSS) ├── file-integrity-operator/ # AIDE file integrity monitoring ├── cert-manager/ # TLS certificate lifecycle management ├── gatekeeper/ # OPA Gatekeeper policy enforcement ├── network-policies/ # Default-deny network policies with allowlists ├── nmstate-operator/ # NMState node network configuration ├── external-dns/ # Automated DNS record management ├── ingress-controller/ # Custom ingress controller tuning ├── multus-networks/ # Multus secondary CNI networks ├── network-observability/ # eBPF Network Observability with FlowCollector ├── alertmanager-config/ # Alertmanager routing (Slack/PagerDuty/Email) ├── custom-grafana-dashboards/ # Grafana dashboards (capacity/GPU/namespace) ├── opentelemetry-collector/ # OpenTelemetry distributed tracing with Tempo ├── loki-logging/ # LokiStack log aggregation ├── thanos-ruler/ # Thanos long-term metrics storage ├── node-tuning-profiles/ # Performance tuning (hugepages/RT kernel/sysctl) ├── image-registry/ # Internal image registry with pruning ├── custom-catalogsource/ # Private operator catalog sources ├── machine-config-pools/ # Custom MachineConfigPools for worker groups ├── node-maintenance/ # Node Maintenance Operator for controlled drain ├── cost-management/ # Red Hat Cost Management metrics ├── devspaces/ # Eclipse Che / OpenShift Dev Spaces ├── web-terminal/ # Browser-based web terminal ├── image-streams/ # Custom ImageStreams and S2I refresh ├── kuberay-operator/ # KubeRay for Ray cluster orchestration ├── training-operator/ # Kubeflow Training Operator (PyTorch/TF) ├── model-registry/ # ML model versioning registry ├── nvidia-nim/ # NVIDIA NIM inference microservices ├── mig-manager/ # NVIDIA MIG GPU partitioning ├── global-load-balancer/ # Cross-cluster global load balancing ├── velero-schedule/ # Velero backup schedules └── dr-runbook-automation/ # DR failover/failback automation 
## Cross-Cluster Connectivity — DC Primary

The DC Primary cluster has connections to all three other clusters:

![DC Primary Connectivity](../diagrams/clusters/02-dc-primary-connectivity.svg){: .drawio-diagram }

???+ note "Draw.io Source: DC Primary Connectivity"
    [:material-download: Download .drawio file](../diagrams/clusters/02-dc-primary-connectivity.drawio){ .md-button } — Open in [draw.io](https://app.diagrams.net) for interactive editing.

    <details>
    <summary>View original Mermaid source</summary>

    ```mermaid
graph TB
    subgraph DC_PRIMARY["DC Primary Workload Cluster"]
        OCP["OCP 4.15 Baremetal"]
        SUB_BROKER["Submariner<br/><b>Broker</b><br/>:6443 / :8443"]
        SUB_GW["Submariner<br/><b>Gateway</b><br/>:4500 UDP"]
        ODF_CEPH["ODF Ceph Storage<br/>RBD Mirror Daemon<br/>:6789 / :3300"]
        RHOAI["OpenShift AI<br/>Model Serving"]
        GPU["NVIDIA GPU Stack"]
    end

    subgraph DR_SECONDARY["DR Secondary"]
        DR_GW["Submariner Agent<br/><b>Gateway</b>"]
        DR_ODF["ODF Ceph<br/>RBD Mirror"]
    end

    subgraph MGMT_DC_BOX["Mgmt DC"]
        ACM["ACM Hub<br/>Klusterlet Agent"]
        ACS["ACS Central<br/>SecuredCluster Sensor"]
    end

    subgraph MGMT_DR_BOX["Mgmt DR"]
        ACM_DR["ACM Standby"]
    end

    %% Submariner connections
    DR_GW -->|"1 Broker Registration<br/>TCP 6443"| SUB_BROKER
    SUB_GW <-->|"2 IPsec Tunnel<br/>UDP 4500 + ESP"| DR_GW

    %% ODF DR replication
    ODF_CEPH <-->|"3 RBD Mirroring<br/>TCP 6789, 3300"| DR_ODF

    %% ACM management
    ACM -->|"4 Klusterlet Import<br/>TCP 6443"| OCP

    %% ACS scanning
    ACS -->|"5 SecuredCluster<br/>Sensor deployed"| OCP

    style DC_PRIMARY fill:#e8f5e9,stroke:#2e7d32,stroke-width:2px
    style DR_SECONDARY fill:#e3f2fd,stroke:#1565c0
    style MGMT_DC_BOX fill:#fff3e0,stroke:#ef6c00
    style MGMT_DR_BOX fill:#fff3e0,stroke:#ef6c00


</details>
# Connection Direction Protocol Purpose 1 DR Agent → DC Broker Inbound TCP 6443 Submariner agent registers with broker 2 DC Gateway ↔ DR Gateway Bidirectional UDP 4500 + ESP Encrypted pod-to-pod tunnel 3 DC Ceph ↔ DR Ceph Bidirectional TCP 6789, 3300 RBD mirroring (async/sync) 4 ACM Hub → DC API Inbound TCP 6443 Klusterlet agent, policy delivery 5 ACS Central → DC Inbound TCP 443 SecuredCluster sensor deployment

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
Before running Terraform, ensure the following are in place:

Requirement Details Terraform >= 1.5.0 Bastion/Provisioner Node RHEL 8.x/9.x with kni user, libvirt, qemu-kvm, oc CLI, openshift-baremetal-install Pull Secret Downloaded from console.redhat.com DNS A records for api.<cluster>.<domain>, *.apps.<cluster>.<domain>, master/worker nodes, etcd SRV records DHCP MAC-based IP reservations for all cluster nodes BMC Access iDRAC/iLO/XCC Redfish API accessible from provisioner node SSH Key Pair Ed25519 or RSA key pair for core user access Local Quay (disconnected) Quay server with 2TB+ storage, CA certificate, admin credentials NVIDIA NGC (GPU nodes) NGC API key for vGPU driver images NLS License Token (GPU nodes) NVIDIA License Server client token file Red Hat Entitlement PEM For cluster-wide entitlement (vGPU driver builds)

docs.title: Firewall Port Requirements
docs.text: The following ports must be opened in the firewall between the relevant network zones:

docs.title: Cluster Communication Ports
docs.text: Port Protocol Source Destination Purpose 6443 TCP Clients / Load Balancer API VIP / Control Plane Kubernetes API Server 22623 TCP Cluster Nodes API VIP / Control Plane Machine Config Server 443 TCP Clients / Load Balancer Apps VIP / Workers HTTPS Ingress (Router) 80 TCP Clients / Load Balancer Apps VIP / Workers HTTP Ingress (Router) 2379-2380 TCP Control Plane Control Plane etcd peer and client 10250 TCP Control Plane All Nodes Kubelet API 10257 TCP Control Plane Control Plane kube-controller-manager 10259 TCP Control Plane Control Plane kube-scheduler 9000-9999 TCP/UDP All Nodes All Nodes Host-level services (node exporter, etc.) 30000-32767 TCP/UDP All Nodes All Nodes NodePort Services (Kubernetes) 4789 UDP All Nodes All Nodes VXLAN (OVN-Kubernetes overlay) 6081 UDP All Nodes All Nodes Geneve (OVN-Kubernetes overlay) 500 UDP All Nodes All Nodes IPsec IKE (if encryption enabled) 4500 UDP All Nodes All Nodes IPsec NAT-T (if encryption enabled)

docs.title: Infrastructure Services Ports
docs.text: Port Protocol Source Destination Purpose 53 TCP/UDP All Nodes DNS Server DNS resolution 123 UDP All Nodes NTP Server Time synchronization 22 TCP Bastion All Nodes / BMC SSH access 443 TCP Bastion BMC (iDRAC/iLO/XCC) Redfish API (bare metal provisioning) 5000 TCP Bastion Provisioning Network RHCOS image HTTP server 67-69 UDP DHCP/TFTP Server Provisioning Network DHCP/TFTP (PXE boot — UPI)

docs.title: Quay Registry & Operator Mirroring Ports
docs.text: Port Protocol Source Destination Purpose 443 TCP Local Quay cdn.redhat.com / quay.io Pull operator images from Red Hat CDN 8443 TCP Bastion / Cluster Nodes Local Quay (internet-facing) Pull mirrored images from local Quay 443 TCP Bastion Quay Enterprise Route (mgmt clusters) Push replicated images to Quay Enterprise 443 TCP Workload Cluster Nodes Quay Enterprise Route (mgmt clusters) Pull operator images from Quay Enterprise

docs.title: Storage (ODF/Ceph) Ports
docs.text: Port Protocol Source Destination Purpose 6789 TCP ODF Nodes ODF Nodes Ceph Monitor 3300 TCP ODF Nodes ODF Nodes Ceph Monitor (v2) 6800-7300 TCP ODF Nodes ODF Nodes Ceph OSD / MDS / RGW

docs.title: Submariner (Multi-Cluster Networking) Ports
docs.text: Port Protocol Source Destination Purpose 4500 UDP Gateway Nodes (all clusters) Gateway Nodes (all clusters) Submariner IPsec NAT-T 500 UDP Gateway Nodes (all clusters) Gateway Nodes (all clusters) Submariner IPsec IKE ESP (IP 50) IP Gateway Nodes Gateway Nodes Submariner IPsec ESP 4800 UDP All Nodes (all clusters) All Nodes (all clusters) Submariner VXLAN tunnel 8080 TCP All Nodes Submariner Gateway Submariner health check

docs.title: GPU & AI/ML Ports
docs.text:
Port Protocol Source Destination Purpose 443 TCP GPU Nodes nls.nvidia.com NVIDIA License Server (cloud) 443 TCP GPU Nodes NGC Container Registry NVIDIA GPU Operator images

Disconnected / Air-Gapped Environments

In air-gapped deployments, the only system with internet access should be the Local Quay (internet-facing mirror). All other cluster nodes must pull images from local Quay or Quay Enterprise. Ensure the firewall blocks direct internet access from cluster nodes.

docs.title: Secrets & Credentials — Files on the Bastion Host
docs.text:
Download and place the following files on the bastion node before running Terraform:

File Bastion Path Download Source Terraform Variable Pull Secret /home/kni/pull-secret.json console.redhat.com/openshift/downloads — requires Red Hat account pull_secret_file SSH Private Key /home/kni/.ssh/id_ed25519 Generate: ssh-keygen -t ed25519 -f /home/kni/.ssh/id_ed25519 -N '' ssh_private_key_file SSH Public Key /home/kni/.ssh/id_ed25519.pub Generated with private key ssh_public_key_file Quay CA Certificate /home/kni/quay-ca.crt Export from your Quay mirror server's TLS CA chain quay_ca_cert_file NLS License Token /home/kni/nls-client-token.tok NVIDIA Licensing Portal → Service Instances → Client Token nls_token_file Entitlement PEM /home/kni/entitlement.pem Red Hat Subscription Manager: subscription-manager register && subscription-manager attach entitlement_pem_file

File Permissions

All secret files should be owned by the kni user with restricted permissions: 

chmod 600 /home/kni/pull-secret.json
chmod 600 /home/kni/.ssh/id_ed25519
chmod 600 /home/kni/nls-client-token.tok
chmod 644 /home/kni/quay-ca.crt

docs.title: Secrets & Credentials — ADO Variable Group
docs.text:
Create a Variable Group named ocp-baremetal-secrets in Azure DevOps (Project Settings → Pipelines → Library → + Variable Group):

Secret Name Source Used By Description quay-admin-password Your Quay mirror admin password All clusters Quay mirror registry authentication ngc-api-key NVIDIA NGC Portal → Generate API Key DC, DR Pull vGPU driver images from nvcr.io submariner-broker-token From DC Primary after Submariner broker install:oc get secret -n submariner-k8s-broker DR Cross-cluster Submariner tunnel odf-dr-s3-access-key S3-compatible storage (MinIO, AWS, etc.) for ODF DR metadata store DR ODF DR async replication odf-dr-s3-secret-key Same S3 storage provider DR ODF DR async replication acs-central-admin-password Choose a strong password (min 12 chars) Mgmt DC ACS StackRox Central initial admin acm-s3-access-key S3-compatible storage for ACM Observability Thanos store Mgmt DC, Mgmt DR ACM Hub Observability acm-s3-secret-key Same S3 storage provider Mgmt DC, Mgmt DR ACM Hub Observability

Security Best Practices

  • Mark all secrets as 🔒 secret (padlock icon) in ADO to mask them in pipeline logs
  • Never commit secrets to Git — use ADO Variable Groups or HashiCorp Vault
  • Rotate ngc-api-key and S3 keys periodically
  • Use separate S3 buckets for ODF DR and ACM Observability
